data.title: 20 Crypto Hacks Occurred in February, Losing About $1.51 Billion
data.description: According to PeckShieldAlert, there were 20 cryptocurrency hacker attacks in February, resulting in a loss of approximately $1.51 billion, making it the most loss-making month in the history of cryptocurrency. The Bybit incident alone lost $1.43 billion, ranking first in the list of the largest intrusion incidents in the past five years.

data.title: Judge Tosses SEC’s Case Against Richard Heart—But He Remains on the Run
data.description:
A U.S. district judge has dismissed the SEC’s lawsuit against Hex founder Richard Heart, citing lack of jurisdiction, but Heart still faces legal troubles in Finland.

The post Judge Tosses SEC’s Case Against Richard Heart—But He Remains on the Run first appeared on Ecoinimist.

source: https://ecoinimist.com/2025/03/01/sec-drops-case-against-richard-heart/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=sec-drops-case-against-richard-heart

data.title: Legendary rapper Snoop Dogg announces he will no longer use Spotify and will switch to Web3 streaming platform
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Legendary rapper Snoop Dogg announced that he will no longer use Spotify (I don't f**k with Spotify anymore) and turn to Web3 streaming platform. It is reported that Snoop Dogg has reached a cooperation with Tune.fm to release the single "Spaceship Party", but a Tune.fm spokesperson refused to disclose more transaction details of the cooperation between the two parties. (Billboard)
Previously, the decentralized music streaming platform Tune.fm completed a $50 million financing in September last year, with LDA Capital, Alpha Token Capital, Block Alpha, Animoca Brands, The HBAR Foundation and GDA Capital participating in the investment.

data.title: Olumide Osunkoya Sentenced to 4 Years for £2.5M Illegal ATM Scheme
data.description:
Olumide Osunkoya was sentenced to four years in prison for running unregistered cryptocurrency ATMs in the UK, despite being denied FCA approval.
source: https://coincu.com/324827-olumide-osunkoya-sentenced-to-4-years
